Patch Set 18: Code-Review+1

Hi,

I found this patch because some CEA's admins complain about parsing the "lfs quota" output. This patch is nice.

2 more things that would be great:
- An option to suppress the header line (--quiet or --no-header).
- An option to specify a custom char delimiter (--delimiter). We had some issues in the past with field displayed with an empty string (I think that was grace period for some cases, but I am not sure).

Patch Set 18:

> - An option to specify a custom char delimiter (--delimiter). We had some issues in the past with field displayed with an empty string (I think that was grace period for some cases, but I am not sure).

--quiet does in fact already exist and suppresses the header line, the delimiter option is a good idea, I'll put out a separate patch including that.

Patch Set 20: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

1. I haven't finished inspecting the whole code. However there is at least one significant problem why it can't be landed. Options "--block-softlimit|--bsoftlimit" and "--block-hardlimit|--bhardlimit" don't work:
[root@vm1 utils]# lfs quota -u quota_usr --bhardlimit /mnt/lustre
lfs quota: unrecognized option '--bhardlimit'
Display disk usage and limits.
usage: quota [-q] [-v] [-h] [-o OBD_UUID|-o OST_IDX|-m MDT_IDX]
             [{-u|-g|-p} UNAME|UID|GNAME|GID|PROJID]
             [--pool OST_POOL_NAME]
             [--filesystem|--mount-point]
             [--blocks|--busage|--space]
             [--block-softlimit|--bsoftlimit]
             [--block-hardlimit|--bhardlimit]
             [--block-grace|--bgrace|--btime]
             [--inodes|--iusage]
             [--inode-softlimit|--isoftlimit]
             [--inode-hardlimit|--ihardlimit]
             [--inode-grace|--igrace|--itime]
             [MOUNT_POINT ...]
       quota -t {-u|-g|-p} [--pool OST_POOL_NAME] [MOUNT_POINT ...]
       quota [-hqv] {-U|-G|-P} [--pool OST_POOL_NAME] [MOUNT_POINT ...]
       quota -a {-u|-g|-p} [-s START_QID] [-e END_QID] [MOUNT_POINT ...]

2. Ideally it should have a simple test case in sanity-quota.sh that will check each newly added parameter. This would help to avoid problems I described in the 1st item.

parents.subject: LU-17848 osd: move fid_is_fs_root() to lustre_fid.h

commit.subject: LU-18079 utils: argument parse opts for lfs quota
commit.message:
LU-18079 utils: argument parse opts for lfs quota

Adding new options to only get certain arguments from 'lfs quota'.
This works much like 'lfs getstripe'. Each option will show their
respected argument and can be combined together to show multiple
arguments. They will be shown in the same order that they appear
in the full table for 'lfs quota'.
Added:
    --filesystem|--mount-point
    --blocks|--space|--busage
    --block-softlimit|--bsoftlimit
    --block-hardlimit|--bhardlimit
    --block-grace|--bgrace|--btime
    --inodes|--iusage
    --inode-softlimit|--isoftlimit
    --inode-hardlimit|--ihardlimit
    --inode-grace|--igrace|--itime
each matching a column of the table.

Signed-off-by: Frederick Dilger <fdilger@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Iffb9be9a55368a3faad3a8e74ee2f2a6b1ec7301
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55894
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Sergey Cheremencev <scherementsev@ddn.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
